content: We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs.rss We Work Remotely: Remote jobs in design, programming, marketing and more en-US 60 Mathmo: Maths Coach Anywhere in the World London All Other Remote Contract <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/5783/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> London <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="http://www.mathmo.com">http://www.mathmo.com</a> </p> <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">We're looking for incredible mathematicians with genuine personality. That's the whole brief. If you have a top maths degree and people find you easy to talk to, read on.</p> <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">The role is four live Zoom sessions a week with GCSE students across the UK. Foundation sessions run on Tuesday evenings (5-8pm UK time), Higher sessions on Sunday mornings (11am-1:30pm UK time). Ideally you'd be free for both, but if only one works for you, get in touch anyway - we can be flexible.</p> <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">There's also scope for one-to-one coaching alongside the group sessions (further down the line).</p> <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Pay is £75 per hour for sessions and £40 per hour for additional work like resource building or prep (again further down the line if needed).</p> <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">You need to be comfortable with higher-tier GCSE and A Level, have a natural way with teenagers, and genuinely care about education - not just the maths, but the actual process of helping young people understand it. The sessions are genuinely good fun and the students are engaged.</p> <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">You can be based anywhere, but you'll need to know the UK GCSE system well and be reliably available at the session times above.</p> <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">The role is freelance to start. You can begin before the end of term or over the summer, with the aim of building into regular work from September.</p> <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Mathmo is growing fast. GCSE is the start - A Level, 11+, SATs and more are all coming. If you want to be part of building something in education that's actually working, apply here: <a class="underline underline underline-offset-2 decoration-1 decoration-current/40 hover:decoration-current focus:decoration-current" href="https://forms.gle/cCzPxF3v6tuuDvnM6">https://forms.gle/cCzPxF3v6tuuDvnM6</a></p> <p><strong>To apply:</strong> <a href="https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mathmo-maths-coach">https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mathmo-maths-coach</a></p> Fri, 10 Jul 2026 22:45:30 +0000 Sun, 09 Aug 2026 22:45:30 +0000 https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mathmo-maths-coach https://weworkremotely.com/remote-jobs/mathmo-maths-coach Qase: Developer Advocate Anywhere in the World Delaware Sales and Marketing Full-Time <img src="https://we-work-remotely.imgix.net/logos/0171/3015/logo.gif?ixlib=rails-4.0.0&w=50&h=50&dpr=2&fit=fill&auto=compress" /> <p> <strong>Headquarters:</strong> Delaware, USA <br /><strong>URL:</strong> <a href="https://qase.io/">https://qase.io/</a> </p> <p><strong>About the role</strong></p> <p>Developers are shipping AI-generated code faster than anyone can review it. Quality is the new bottleneck and AI-generated-code governance is increasingly necessary. Qase is how engineering teams verify the quality of AI-generated code, so their companies can safely adopt AI-accelerated workflows. We're looking for someone with a technical background and love of software quality who wants to contribute to re-imagining how engineering orgs work given the massive implications of heavy adoption of AI-coding platforms and MCP-mediated access to other developer tools.</p> <p><strong>You might be a fit if you're…</strong></p> <p>A talented QA / quality engineer or quality-obsessed developer. From there, two paths fit this role:</p> <ul> <li><strong>A QA practitioner growing into advocacy:</strong>&nbsp;Maybe you've dabbled (a talk, a blog, an OSS contribution), maybe you haven't yet, but you genuinely&nbsp;<em>want</em>&nbsp;to do this: get on stage, work a booth, be in the testing/dev communities. We'll build the advocacy with you: mentorship, speaker coaching, and a staged ramp at real events. You're ready to do it full-time, with a product to influence and a company behind you.</li> <li><strong>Experienced developer advocate:</strong>&nbsp;Speaking at conferences, maintaining or contributing to OSS testing tools, running a newsletter or blog, or the person whose takes on testing and AI-generated code people actually repost,&nbsp;<em>and</em>&nbsp;with the QA credibility to back it. You arrive ready and ramp faster.</li> </ul> <p>You're credible because you've done the work, not because you read about it.</p> <p><strong>What you'll do</strong></p> <ul> <li>Be part of the communities we serve: online (the testing/dev Discords, Slacks, subreddits, LinkedIn), at meetups, and on conference stages. Showing up in these communities as a peer who happens to work at Qase and who sincerely wants to help their peers find ways to improve their workflow.</li> <li>Take a real point of view on software quality in the age of AI: how teams should test AI-generated code, verify intent, and keep token use under control. Start with thoughtful research, and from there create derivative content and genuinely-useful tools.</li> <li>Make the rest of the company credible. Coach sales and marketing on what lands and what gets an eye-roll. e.g. be the person a salesperson can grab before a call to understand the difference between a release manager and an SDET.</li> <li>Run the US field motion: practitioner meetups, prospect dinners and outings, conference presence, alongside reps and a supporting event coordinator.</li> <li>Close the loop to product. You'll understand what the community wants, and represent their needs to the rest of Qase.</li> </ul> <p><strong>What we're looking for</strong></p> <p>Required of every candidate:</p> <ul> <li><strong>QA / software development credibility.</strong>&nbsp;A testing or developer audience trusts you.</li> <li><strong>A genuine desire to evangelize.</strong>&nbsp;You&nbsp;<em>want</em>&nbsp;to be on stage and at the booth. Not tolerate it, want it. No desire to present is not a fit.</li> <li>A commercial instinct. You think about whether the work grows the business, not only whether it's interesting. Be entrepreneurial, and keep an eye out for opportunities to turn experimental projects into durable and scalable DevRel programs.</li> <li>Hands-on technical depth. You've written tests, broken builds, and used AI coding tools enough to have opinions about where they're great as well as what their limitations are.</li> <li>Comfort with ambiguity and porous boundaries. This is a series A startup, and the "how" will change often. We're looking to hire an adaptable problem so
